prompts: Earthquakes are caused by the movement of tectonic plates, which are large sections of Earth's crust. These plates can move in different directions and interact at points known as fault lines. The stress and pressure from these movements accumulate at the faults until they suddenly release energy, causing the ground to shake. This released energy travels as seismic waves, which spread outward from the point of rupture, shaking the ground and potentially causing damage. The most common types of tectonic interactions leading to earthquakes include convergent, divergent, and transform plate boundaries.
